summary: Soundboard source with good‑to‑great clarity captures a late‑’90s show highlighted by a monster Playing In The Band, Here Comes Sunshine and tight China Doll performances.
review: The Miller SBD transfer is generally clear and well balanced, though occasional muffled bits and heavy editing of the Drums/Space segment are noted. Musically the concert shines with an inspired Playing In The Band, a soaring Here Comes Sunshine and superb guitar work from Jerry, while a few mid‑set numbers feel merely average for ’93.

ai_rating.rationale: Over ten detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (especially PITB, Here Comes Sunshine and China Doll) and rate the recording as solid; only minor technical complaints keep the score just above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp overall sound with a nice mix; some reviewers note muffled/faint sections and heavy editing of Drums>Space, but the CM‑enhanced transfer is praised as listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set mixes rare gems (Samson, Broken Arrow, Ramble on Rose) with solid staples; the second set builds to a peak with Playing In The Band and its reprise, delivering a classic late‑era jam despite some average moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for pleasant guitar tones, creative MIDI/wah‑wah work and superb fretwork throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for intriguing string nuances on bass.
band_member_comments.Bob: Slightly hoarse vocal on a few songs; occasional lyric flubs and a missed verse on Bobby's singing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboardist) sings and plays competently, adding solid support.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers “well acquit themselves”; Drums/Space segment heavily edited but still energetic.
